Per-View DCF Overfit — Experiment Report

20200709-subject-01/20200709_141754 (63 tracked frames)

ACP om-h6c7mha7 · 500 epochs · 1 sample · 4 views · SPOT · 19.8 min
Status
CONVERGED SVD STABLE

TL;DR

Per-view DCF architecture fix stopped the rank collapse. The aggregator now broadcasts the canonical DCF extra tokens along the view dimension, and DCFBranch.decode_outputs emits a per-view trajectory [B, S, N_h, 3] aligned with the main branch's per-view pose/depth/world-points outputs. The failed 2026-04-16 overfit collapsed hand SVDs3/s1 to 0.077; this run converges with a stable SVD 0.355 at step 500, and training hand L1 settles to 15.6 mm/coord.
Single-frame inference (S=1) on 63 tracked frames reports mean hand L2 124.2 mm / object L2 72.0 mm, chamfer 125.1 / 80.0 mm. Training saw 4-view strided context; S=1 eval removes that context and is therefore a floor — the camera-view overlays and 3D scatters below are the honest quality signal.

Camera-View Overlay

Predicted hand/object/scene projected into the camera plane via the sequence intrinsics. Hand points are blue, object points are green, GT is gray, scene pointmap is rendered as thin dots.
